law_time_entries.description: Review construction contract and specifications. Identify breach provisions and damages calculation methodology. Prepare initial case assessment memo.
Draft asset purchase agreement. Review target company IP portfolio and employment agreements. Conference call with client CFO regarding earn-out structure.
Due diligence document review. Organize corporate records, operating agreements, and secretary of state filings for target entity. Prepare due diligence index.
Review purchase and sale agreement. Identify title issues from preliminary title report. Prepare rider with attorney approval contingency language.
Client intake meeting. Review termination letter and employment agreement. Discuss potential claims under state wrongful termination statute and federal FMLA.
Review opposing counsel's revised property division proposal. Prepare counter-proposal with updated asset valuations. Telephone conference with client to discuss settlement strategy.
Draft revocable living trust agreement. Prepare pour-over will, durable power of attorney, and healthcare directive. Review beneficiary designations on retirement accounts.
Firm administrative meeting. CLE planning for Q2.

law_deadlines.title: Statute of Limitations — Breach of Contract
law_deadlines.notes: 6-year SOL from date of breach (July 15, 2020). Must file complaint before this date.

law_deadlines.title: File Initial Complaint
law_deadlines.notes: Internal deadline. File well before SOL expiration for safe harbor.

law_deadlines.title: Due Diligence Completion
law_deadlines.notes: Per LOI terms, buyer must complete due diligence by this date or request extension.

law_deadlines.title: Attorney Review Period Expiration
law_deadlines.notes: 3 business-day attorney review period. Must send disapproval letter or rider before expiration.

law_deadlines.title: EEOC Charge Filing Deadline
law_deadlines.notes: 180-day filing deadline from date of termination (Feb 20, 2026). Must file EEOC charge before pursuing federal claims.

law_deadlines.title: Mediation Session
law_deadlines.notes: Court-ordered mediation. Prepare settlement summary and financial disclosure update.

law_deadlines.title: Response to Employer's Position Statement
law_deadlines.notes: Expected timeline after EEOC charge is filed. Prepare detailed rebuttal with documentation.

law_documents.title: Initial Case Assessment — Reynolds v. Pinnacle
law_documents.summary: Analysis of breach of contract and construction defect claims. Reviews contract terms, scope of defective work, and estimated damages of $340,000 including remediation costs and lost rental income.
law_documents.author: David Park, Esq.

law_documents.title: Asset Purchase Agreement — Brighten Tech
law_documents.summary: Draft APA for acquisition of Brighten Technologies by client. Covers asset identification, purchase price ($2.4M plus earn-out), representations, warranties, indemnification, and closing conditions.
law_documents.author: David Park, Esq.

law_documents.title: Due Diligence Checklist and Index
law_documents.summary: Comprehensive due diligence checklist covering corporate records, IP portfolio, material contracts, litigation history, financial statements, and employment agreements for Brighten Technologies acquisition.
law_documents.author: Jennifer Walsh, Paralegal

law_documents.title: Purchase Agreement Rider — Lopez Residence
law_documents.summary: Attorney review rider modifying standard residential purchase agreement. Adds inspection contingency extension, radon testing requirement, and title cure period from 30 to 60 days.
law_documents.author: Maria Gonzalez, Esq.

law_documents.title: Henderson Revocable Living Trust
law_documents.summary: Revocable living trust for Robert and Patricia Henderson. Includes pour-over provisions, successor trustee designations, and distribution schedule for three adult children with staggered distributions.
law_documents.author: Maria Gonzalez, Esq.

law_documents.title: Settlement Counter-Proposal — Chen Divorce
law_documents.summary: Counter-proposal to opposing counsel's property division. Proposes 55/45 split based on updated home appraisal and client's non-marital property contributions. Includes revised spousal support schedule.
law_documents.author: Maria Gonzalez, Esq.

law_documents.title: Wrongful Termination Demand Letter
law_documents.summary: Pre-litigation demand letter to former employer alleging wrongful termination in violation of FMLA and state law. Demands reinstatement, back pay, and compensatory damages of $185,000.
law_documents.author: David Park, Esq.

law_trust_accounts.description: Initial retainer deposit for acquisition matter
Transfer for February billing — 5.8 hours at $425/hr plus paralegal time
Initial retainer deposit for wrongful termination matter
Initial retainer deposit for divorce matter
Transfer for December billing — 5.0 hours at $325/hr
Transfer for January billing — 3.9 hours at $325/hr (0.1 hour non-billable written off)
Flat fee deposit for residential purchase closing representation
